title: getParent()
text: getParent() Returns an Uniface JavaScript object that represents the parent of the current object. Object . getParent() Arguments None Return Value Returns one of: null If Object is a uniface.Instance (a top-level object) uniface.Instance If Object is a uniface.Entity . uniface.Entity If Object is a uniface.Occurrence . uniface.Occurrence If Object is a uniface.Field . Exceptions The following exceptions may be thrown: uniface.InvalidDataObjectException Example: Get the Parent Occurrence of a Field The parent a field object is an occurrence. The this keyword always refers to the current object (in this case, the field), so to get its parent occurrence, use this. getParent() . For example: Explain this code webtrigger detail ; of field javascript this.getParent().remove(); ; remove the occurrence (marking it for deletion if it exists on the server) endjavascript end Copy Related Links uniface.InvalidDataObjectException Exception thrown if the object on which the function is called is no longer valid. uniface.Instance The uniface.Instance object provides access to selected properties and methods of the component instance that it represents. uniface.Occurrence The uniface.Occurrence object provides access to selected properties of the occurrence that it represents. It is returned by the getOccurrence function of the uniface.Entity object. uniface.Entity The uniface.Entity object provides access to selected properties of the component entity that it represents. It is returned by the getEntities and getEntity functions of the uniface.Instance and uniface.Occurrence objects. uniface.Field The uniface.Field object provides access to selected properties of the field that it represents. It is returned by the getField function of uniface.Occurrence. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
